project_summary: The project aims to capture the main requirements and constraints for the 2005 - microcare document management system.

functional.text: When creating a new staff member, if a staff with an existing name is entered, the system must generate a warning message.
The system must allow a user to save a new staff member with an existing name if required.
A user must be able to mark a claim's status as 'Rejected'.
The system must allow a rejected claim to be sent back to the VSP.
The receipt must be based on information from the claim submission.
The receipt must automatically list the details for each voucher used.
The system needs to provide the flexibility to create any number of individual users under any of the user groups.
During every distribution transaction, the user must select the name of the salesman from the Team Master list.
The system needs a place to enter payment terms agreed upon between MSIU and the VSP, which are then displayed on the VSP Master Information screen.

non_functional.text: Only an authorized user can alter or amend a claim.
During the claim entry process, the system must capture the necessary information about what's happening.
The voucher validity date can only be amended or altered at the system level by an authorized user.
The details for each voucher used on a receipt should be shown in a grid format.
Only a system admin can create User Groups.

data.text: The system needs to have a 'Salesman master' record that captures all the necessary information for each person.
The 'Salesman master' record must capture all necessary information for each person.
Each voucher slip should have a bar code with two identifications, one for the client and one for the partner.
The value for 'Health Sub – District' must be selected from a master list.
When a user is entering VSP details, the value for 'Locality' should come from its own master list.
When a user is entering VSP details, the value for 'Level Of Facility' should come from its own master list.
When a user is entering VSP details, the value for 'Registration Body' should come from its own master list.
The voucher will have an MRP, or maximum retail price.
The receipt needs to automatically list the details for each voucher used, pulling the `Batch No` and `voucher No` from the available voucher stock.
The receipt must include the `Patient Status`.
The `Patient Status` must be selected from the `Patient Status Master` list.
The `Reason for Referral` must be populated from the `Referral master`.
The value for 'Locality' must be selected from a master list.
The value for 'Level Of Facility' must be selected from a master list.

business_rules.text: The invoice amount shall be calculated as the number of vouchers multiplied by the wholesale price.
For transactions where the invoice amount is the number of vouchers multiplied by the wholesale price, the mode of payment is always Cash.
If the `Patient Status` is 'Referral', the receipt must capture who the patient was `Referred To`.
If a claim from the same VSP has a thumb impression mismatch more than two times, the system must in-activate that VSP.

interfaces.text: Each voucher slip must have a bar code.

constraints.text: All rules for the voucher, including health care services and quality standards for providers, are defined in the PDS.
The physical voucher must be designed with three tear-off portioned slips.
One of the voucher's tear-off slips must have a sub-section tear-off for the partner.
